summary: The US and Iran traded strikes in the Gulf today, testing a fragile two-month-old ceasefire. The US military said it hit Iranian drones and radar sites, and Iran retaliated by targeting US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. The escalation raises the risk of a broader conflict in a region already on edge. Meanwhile, Israel continued bombarding Hezbollah targets in Lebanon as cease-fire efforts stalled, and US Central Command reported intercepting seven ballistic missiles fired toward Kuwait and Bahrain. The upshot: the ceasefire that had brought some calm is now under serious strain, and the US is directly involved in two theaters of conflict at once.

headlines.title: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf in latest test of ceasefire
headlines.summary: The US military struck Iranian drones and radar sites; Iran retaliated by targeting US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. Further escalation threatens the two-month-old ceasefire.

framings.angle: Led with US strike and Iranian retaliation details
Covered US intercepting Iranian attacks alongside Israel's Lebanon bombings
Reported Bahrain denouncing 'blatant aggression' from Iran

headlines.title: CDC warns Ebola in central Africa could match 2014 record outbreak
headlines.summary: US CDC computer models project 10,000 to over 20,000 cases; the 2014-2016 west Africa outbreak had more than 28,000 cases. Health officials urge rapid containment measures.

headlines.title: Israel continues bombarding Lebanon as US intercepts Iranian missiles
headlines.summary: US Central Command said seven ballistic missiles were fired toward Kuwait and Bahrain. Meanwhile, Israel maintains strikes on Hezbollah targets in Lebanon as cease-fire efforts falter.

framings.angle: Covered dual fronts: US-Iran and Israel-Hezbollah
Focused on cease-fire hopes fading as fighting continues

headlines.title: Civilians flee Mogadishu as Somali troops and opposition militias clash
headlines.summary: Fierce fighting erupted in Somalia's capital before planned protests over the president staying in office after his term expired. Property was damaged and civilians displaced.

headlines.title: Singapore orders social media platforms to block posts targeting Indian community
headlines.summary: Police issued directions under the Online Criminal Harms Act to block 14 posts on YouTube, Facebook and X that undermine multiculturalism. Platforms must disable access for Singapore users.

headlines.title: PLA stays on high alert after Dutch warship crosses Taiwan Strait
headlines.summary: The People's Liberation Army tracked the Dutch frigate De Ruyter through the Taiwan Strait. The PLA said the ship's helicopter illegally entered airspace near China's Xisha islands.

headlines.title: US imposes new sanctions on Cuban president and Castro family members
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced fresh economic sanctions targeting Cuba's president, his immediate family, and members of the Castro family. Entities providing services are at risk.

headlines.title: Kim Jong Un orders North Korean navy to build 10,000-tonne destroyer
headlines.summary: State media reported Kim Jong Un visited military sites before a visit by China's Xi Jinping. The order signals continued military modernization despite diplomatic engagements.

headlines.title: Peru heads to runoff election between Fujimori and Sanchez
headlines.summary: Sunday's presidential runoff pits Keiko Fujimori against Roberto Sanchez amid social tension, rising crime and declining trust in institutions. The vote marks a decade of political turmoil.

headlines.title: Africa CDC and WHO launch joint $518 million Ebola response plan
headlines.summary: The joint continental plan aims to raise US$518 million to support African countries in preparing for, rapidly detecting and responding to the ongoing Bundibugyo virus outbreak.
